commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=fbb453536588933442e8d7b56955f6c2788a345b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a new `agents.defaults.compaction.fallbacks` config field, wires it into embedded compaction fallback resolution, adds focused tests, and documents the fallback order.

PR surface: Source +28, Tests +135, Docs +25. Total +188 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source-level review finding: an explicit `compaction.model` returns before the fallback wrapper, so the documented `compaction.model` plus `compaction.fallbacks` config cannot reach the new resolver. I did not run a live provider quota scenario.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review and contributor repair are needed because the draft branch is conflicting, changes a compatibility-sensitive config/provider-routing surface, lacks real behavior proof, and has blocking review findings.

target.title: feat(compaction): add compaction.fallbacks: string[] for ordered model fallback chain
target.body:
## Summary

Adds `agents.defaults.compaction.fallbacks: string[]` — an ordered list of provider/model references to retry when the compaction summarization model fails with a quota, rate-limit, or transient provider error.

```jsonc
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "compaction": {
        "model": "minimax/MiniMax-M3",
        "fallbacks": [
          "openai/gpt-5.4",
          "deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash"
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Motivation

`compaction.model` exists and works. But it points to a single model. If that model 404s, rate-limits, or hits quota, compaction silently falls back to truncate-only — the session keeps running, but no LLM-generated summary is produced. From the user's side it looks like `/compact` and auto-compaction did nothing.

The chat model already has `agents.defaults.model.fallbacks`. Compaction is a different concern — it often runs on a cheaper or different provider — so it needs its own chain.

## Changes

**`src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts`** — `fallbacks?: string[]` on `AgentCompactionConfig` with resolution-order docs in the JSDoc.

**`src/config/zod-schema.agent-defaults.ts`** — `fallbacks: z.array(z.string()).optional()` in the compaction schema.

**`src/config/schema.help.ts`** — help tooltip for `agents.defaults.compaction.fallbacks`.

**`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.ts`** — `resolveCompactionFallbacksOverride` checks `compaction.fallbacks` before falling through to the chat model's fallback chain. Returns the explicit list when non-empty; empty array is treated as unset.

**`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts`** — 2 new tests:
- Compaction fallbacks chain is preferred over chat model fallbacks (negative-control: chat fallback is NOT tried when compaction.fallbacks is set)
- Empty `compaction.fallbacks[]` falls through to chat fallback chain

**`docs/concepts/compaction.md`** — "Compaction fallback chain" section with config example and resolution-order docs.

## Design decisions

- **Explicit list, not implicit chat fallback reuse.** The chat model's fallback chain was the obvious shared target, but compaction runs on a different provider often enough (cheap summarizer vs expensive chat model) that an explicit decoupled field makes sense. The fallback still chains: `compaction.fallbacks` → `model.fallbacks` → session model.
- **Empty-array treated as unset.** A user setting `compaction.fallbacks: []` is explicitly saying "do not override fallbacks" — the chat chain still fires. This avoids a silent-behavior trap where setting an empty array blocks retries entirely.
- **Per-candidate transcriptPolicy is re-derived** for each fallback attempt, so cross-provider sanitization (Anthropic tool-call pairing, Gemini validation) stays correct. This was a known gap in earlier approaches.

## Real behavior proof

```
$ pnpm test -- src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts
 ✓  agents  src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts (69 tests) 8197ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  69 passed (69)
```

The 2 new tests exercise both the explicit-chain path and the empty-array fallthrough. Both pass.

## Coverage / limits

- Per-agent override in `agents.list[i].compaction.fallbacks` is not included — the per-agent overrides PR (#88506, draft, P2) covers that surface. This PR is limited to `agents.defaults.compaction.fallbacks`.
- Six surface files touched, ~200 lines.
- Ref: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/56781

- [x] Marked as AI-assisted
- [ ] Human-run real behavior proof included (test output)
